era_context: Egypt's Old Kingdom, Dynasty IV, in the reign of Khufu or his immediate successor. The age of pyramid construction continues. Mesopotamia remains in the Early Dynastic period (c. 3000–2334 BCE), with city-states competing for regional dominance. The Indus Valley Civilization is in its Early Harappan phase, approaching urban complexity.
geographic_coverage_gaps: East Asia outside the Yellow River valley

events.title: Khufu's reign or early Khafre period; continuation of Old Kingdom pyramid age
events.description: Depending on chronological system, 2691 BCE falls either in the final years of Khufu's reign (c. 2589–2566 BCE in traditional chronology) or in the early years of Khafre's reign. The Great Pyramid of Khufu is either recently completed or has been standing for several decades. The Egyptian state continues to mobilize vast resources for monumental construction, reflecting centralized authority and sophisticated administrative systems.
events.certainty_note: Chronological uncertainty as noted for -2690. The year 2691 BCE is assigned to Dynasty IV based on conventional Egyptological dating, but alternative chronologies place this period differently.

disconfirming_evidence: As with -2690, the chronological placement of this year is subject to significant debate. The traditional dating of Khufu (c. 2589–2566 BCE) and Khafre (c. 2558–2532 BCE) is not universally accepted. Some scholars propose substantially different dates. The year 2691 BCE may fall outside the reigns of both Khufu and Khafre depending on the chronological system used.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 2691 BCE is extremely limited. No contemporary written records from Egypt survive for this year. The record consists entirely of king lists compiled centuries later and archaeological evidence from monuments. For the rest of the world, written records are absent or extremely sparse. This year is essentially a blank slate in the historical record, with only broad era context available.
